questionText: A box of animal stickers has fewer than 50 stickers. Its full factor-pair list has exactly 3 pairs. One factor pair is 2 × 9, and there is no square pair. Which number fits all the clues?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can find all factor pairs of a number up to 100
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Factors, Multiples & Primes: Factors & Multiples, aligned to Students can find all factor pairs of a number up to 100.
explanation: The factor pair 2 × 9 means the number is 18. Now check the other clues. The full factor pairs of 18 are 1 × 18, 2 × 9, and 3 × 6, so it has exactly 3 factor pairs. It also has no square pair because there is no whole number multiplied by itself to make 18. The other choices do not fit: 24 and 42 do not have 2 × 9 as a factor pair, and 36 has a square pair, 6 × 6. So 18 is the only number that matches all the clues.

questionText: Look at these two ways to list all factor pairs for 64 toy cars. Method A: start at 1 and test numbers in order up to 8, making pairs 1×64, 2×32, 4×16, 8×8. Method B: list 1×64, 2×32, 4×16, 8×8, 16×4, 32×2, 64×1. Decide which method is better for a complete factor-pair list and why.
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can find all factor pairs of a number up to 100
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Factors, Multiples & Primes: Factors & Multiples, aligned to Students can find all factor pairs of a number up to 100.
options: [{"text":"Method A is better because it gives each factor pair once and stops at the square pair 8×8.","correct":true},{"text":"Method B is better because a complete list must show every pair in both orders.","correct":false},{"text":"Method A is wrong because it forgot 16×4 and 32×2 as new factor pairs.","correct":false},{"text":"Method B is better because 64 has 7 different factor pairs.","correct":false}]
explanation: Well done! Method A is the better complete list. For 64, the factor pairs are 1×64, 2×32, 4×16, and 8×8. After 8×8, the pairs start repeating in reverse order.

questionText: A box has 18 gummy bears. Choose the factor pair that makes 18.
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can find all factor pairs of a number up to 100
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Factors, Multiples & Primes: Factors & Multiples, aligned to Students can find all factor pairs of a number up to 100.
explanation: Nice work! 2 × 9 = 18, so 2 and 9 are a factor pair of 18. The others do not make 18: 4 × 5 = 20, 3 × 5 = 15, and 6 × 2 = 12.
